title: woof contract-readiness result
description: Output of the deterministic Stage-2.5 contract-readiness node, written to .woof/epics/E<N>/readiness-result.json after EPIC.md exists and before breakdown_planning. `ok` is the conjunction over `checks[].ok` for blocking checks: a check whose severity is `warn` (a non-blocking performance/timeout finding) does not pull `ok` to false. When `ok` is false the graph opens a readiness_gate; when true it records readiness_passed and proceeds to Stage 3.

ok.description: True iff no blocking readiness check failed. warn-severity checks never set this false.

checks.description: One entry per readiness check that ran. The set grows across E2 prompts; consumers key on `id`, never on array length or order.

severity.description: blocker fails readiness; info/minor are advisory passes; warn is a non-blocking performance/timeout finding that never blocks the gate on its own.

summary.description: One-line prose statement of the check outcome.

findings.description: Per-check offending references. Empty or absent when the check passed cleanly.

ref.description: The artefact reference the finding is about: an observable-outcome id (O<n>), contract-decision id (CD<n>), acceptance-criteria index, or a repo-relative path/symbol.

detail.description: Why this reference failed the readiness check.
